prompt:
A single 10 kΩ through-hole resistor lies on an anti-static mat, its cylindrical body with colored bands clearly visible and two straight leads extending outward on either side. The bands are painted in a standard color code pattern but are too small to interpret precisely, emphasizing that this is a typical resistor used in sensor voltage dividers and other circuits. Nearby, a small breadboard and a sensor module hint at how the resistor is used to form a voltage divider.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk electronics workbench with soft, even lighting, neatly arranged components in organizers, and a small plant tucked beside an oscilloscope or multimeter in the background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on equipment, and a composition that centers the resistor and its leads while the surrounding tools and boards remain softly blurred.
